title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Excessive Sweating
intro: Herbal teas for excessive sweating are a natural remedy that involves drinking teas made from various herbs to help alleviate the symptoms of hyperhidrosis, a condition characterized by excessive sweating. These teas can be effective in treating excessive sweating due to their antispasmodic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce sweat production, prevent infections, and promote overall well-being. Examples of herbal teas that can help with excessive sweating include peppermint tea, which cools the body and reduces sweat, chamomile tea, which soothes the nervous system and reduces anxiety-related sweating, and lemongrass tea, which has antifungal properties that can prevent infections. Additionally, other herbal teas such as sage tea, lavender tea, and ginger tea have been found to be effective in reducing sweat production and alleviating symptoms of excessive sweating.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis. The bioactive constituents of ginger, including gingerols and shogaols, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce sweat gland activity and alleviate symptoms of hyperhidrosis. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with excessive sweating. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat hyperhidrosis include reduced sweat production, improved skin health, and a natural, non-invasive approach to managing excessive sweating.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Zingiber officinale root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the root.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea if desired for taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas for excessive sweating is not directly addressed in the study, however, the study does mention the potential use of Z. officinale as a traditional medicinal plant for various diseases, and it is also noted to contain bioactive compounds such as lariciresinol that could inhibit efflux pumps in bacteria, suggesting that it may have potential in treating bacterial infections including those potentially causing excessive sweating, such as methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) which can cause skin infections and excessive sweating in some cases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be beneficial in treating excessive sweating, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to the high content of gingerols, which can irritate the digestive system. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it's recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid taking them on an empty stomach, and drink plenty of water to minimize the risk of stomach upset.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor to confirm the cause of excessive sweating.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale in a cup of hot water.
Drink 2-3 cups a day for 3-4 weeks to observe improvements.
Adjust dosage to 1/2 teaspoon if excessive sweating persists.
Monitor and record sweat levels to track progress and adjust accordingly.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, due to their calming and antispasmodic properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by reducing anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for excessive sweating, and promoting relaxation and calmness. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have a cooling effect on the body and help to reduce sweat production. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat excessive sweating include reduced anxiety and stress levels, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in sweat production, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be a potential remedy for excessive sweating, but they may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, or allergic reactions in some individuals. To safely use Lavandula angustifolia teas, it is essential to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid drinking them before driving or operating heavy machinery to minimize the risk of drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink
Use 1-2 cups per day for 2-4 weeks to see results
Monitor and adjust dosage as needed, and consult a doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating, a condition known as hyperhidrosis. The antispasmodic and anticholinergic properties of Melissa officinalis teas help to reduce sweating by inhibiting the activity of sweat glands. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, including rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the autonomic nervous system and reducing stress-induced sweating. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat excessive sweating include improved quality of life, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and a natural approach to managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it's boiling.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water, then remove from heat.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be effective in treating excessive sweating, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, take it in moderation, avoid consuming it with other medications or herbal preparations, and be cautious when combining it with other treatments that have sedative effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water
Gradually increase dosage to 2-3 teaspoons if needed
Use Melissa officinalis tea consistently for 2-4 weeks
Monitor and adjust dosage or usage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used to treat excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis) due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to reduce sweat gland activity and alleviate symptoms of hyperhidrosis. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the activity of the sympathetic nervous system, which regulates sweat production. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been found to provide relief from excessive sweating, promoting overall well-being and confidence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Add the Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the water and stir well.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals, to help reduce excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat excessive sweating, but they may cause side effects such as water retention, high blood pressure, and an irregular heartbeat in some individuals. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include monitoring blood pressure, being mindful of potassium levels, and avoiding excessive consumption to minimize the risk of these side effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea in morning and evening.
Use 2-3 tablespoons of dried root in 1 cup of boiling water.
Consume tea for 3-4 weeks to see noticeable improvement.
Take 1/2 cup of tea 2-3 times a day for excessive sweating.
Monitor body temperature and adjust tea consumption if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been used traditionally to treat excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, due to their soothing and calming properties that help to reduce anxiety and stress. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates, which have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, thereby reducing excessive sweating. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Valeriana officinalis teas help to alleviate the symptoms of hyperhidrosis, providing relief and comfort to individuals affected by this condition. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat excessive sweating include their natural, non-invasive approach, lack of side effects, and cost-effectiveness, making them a popular alternative remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried root in a tea infuser or directly into a cup.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to treat excessive sweating, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, potentially exacerbating conditions like high blood pressure. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, take them in moderation, ideally in the evening, and avoid driving or operating heavy machinery, as they can induce drowsiness, and also be cautious with mixing them with other medications or substances that may interact with Valeriana officinalis.
preparation_usage: Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root in a cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Adjust the dosage to 2-3 teaspoons for more severe sweating.
Continue using the tea for 2-4 weeks to notice the effects on excessive sweating.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, due to their antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, help to reduce sweat production and alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. By regulating body temperature and preventing the growth of bacteria that thrive in moist environments, Cinnamomum verum teas provide relief from excessive sweating and promote a sense of well-being. The benefits of using Cinnamomum verum teas to treat hyperhidrosis include reduced sweat production, improved skin health, and a decrease in the risk of skin infections and irritation.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 teaspoon of Cinnamomum verum bark powder and put it in a small bowl.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop.
Pour the boiling water over the Cinnamomum verum powder in the bowl.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture using a tea strainer into another cup.
Drink the tea immediately while it's still warm. You can add honey if you want to sweeten it.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, Cinnamomum verum teas, also known as cinnamon, may help reduce excessive sweating, as it has been found to affect lipid profiles, insulin resistance, and blood glucose levels.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum verum teas can be used to treat excessive sweating, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Cinnamomum verum teas safely, it is essential to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, and avoid consuming it with other stimulants or medications that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, due to their ability to regulate body temperature and reduce sweating. The properties of this herbal preparation that help to treat this ailment include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce sweat gland activity. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis, such as quinine and quinidine, have been found to have a direct effect on the sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating and promoting a balanced body temperature. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat excessive sweating include a reduction in sweat stains, improved self-confidence, and a more comfortable and relaxed state of being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona officinalis bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals, to help reduce excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat excessive sweating, but possible side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. Precautions to take when using Cinchona officinalis teas include starting with a low dose to monitor tolerance, avoiding consumption with other medications, and being cautious if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, as excessive consumption may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote a balanced endocrine system. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, caffeic acid, and flavonoids, which help to modulate the body's response to stress and regulate sweat gland function. The benefits of Echinacea purpurea teas in treating excessive sweating include reduced sweat production, improved skin health, and a decrease in anxiety and stress levels. By regulating the body's hormonal balance and promoting a sense of calm, Echinacea purpurea teas provide a natural and effective solution for individuals suffering from hyperhidrosis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers.
Place the measured flowers in the tea infuser or directly into the cup.
Pour boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups throughout the day to help manage excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecular nutrition & food research, Echinacea purpurea teas for excessive sweating may be a safe option, as herbal remedies made from E. purpurea appear to have a low potential to generate cytochrome P450 (CYP 450) drug-herb interactions, indicating a low risk to consumers.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat excessive sweating, but they may cause side effects such as nausea, stomach cramps, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or digestive issues. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid mixing with other medications, and be aware of potential interactions with other herbs or supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula latifolia teas, derived from the flowers of the English lavender plant, have been traditionally used to treat excessive sweating ailments due to their antiseptic, antispasmodic, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, help to regulate body temperature, reduce sweat production, and alleviate symptoms associated with excessive sweating. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Lavandula latifolia teas help to treat excessive sweating ailments by minimizing triggers such as stress and hormonal imbalances. The benefits of using Lavandula latifolia teas to treat excessive sweating include reduced sweat production, improved skin health, and a decrease in the risk of skin infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula latifolia flowers. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula latifolia flowers to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce excessive sweating.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula latifolia teas can be an effective remedy to treat excessive sweating, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and headaches in some individuals. To use Lavandula latifolia teas safely, one should be cautious of interactions with other medications and start with a low dose to monitor individual tolerance, as well as avoid consumption during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

preparation_study: According to the provided study, there is no direct information about Zingiber officinale teas specifically being used for excessive sweating.
